title: do not need timer feature in log tab
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

do not need timer feature in log tab. Delete it.

---

**Category:** `bug` `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
User wants the timer feature removed from the Log tab.

What the user reported: The timer feature in the log tab is unwanted and should be deleted.

What component/area is affected: The Log tab UI — specifically any timer/stopwatch component rendered within the logging interface.

Expected behaviour: The Log tab should not show any timer or stopwatch UI. Users should still be able to log entries (feeds, diapers, sleep, etc.) without a timer widget.

Current behaviour: A timer feature is displayed in the Log tab, which the user finds unnecessary.

Edge cases to handle:
- If any logged data references timer durations, those historical entries should remain intact in storage
- If the timer state is stored anywhere (context, localStorage-equivalent), clean up references but do not delete historical data
- Ensure removing the timer does not break the layout or other log tab functionality

Acceptance criteria:
- Timer UI component is no longer visible in the Log tab
- All other Log tab functionality works as before
- No console errors or broken imports after removal
- Historical entries that used the timer retain their data

Testing:
- Add unit test verifying the Log tab renders without the timer component
- Add test ensuring existing log entries with duration data still display correctly
- Regression test that the Log tab still allows creating new entries after timer removal

title: Real-time family sync with Firebase + end-to-end encryption
body:
## Feature Request

Replace the current manual QR code / share-code sync with automatic real-time sync between family members, powered by Firebase Firestore with end-to-end encryption so no one (including Google/Firebase) can read the data.

---

### Problem

The current sync flow requires manual action every time — open Settings, generate a share code, copy it, send it to partner, partner opens Settings, pastes the code, reviews, and merges. This is tedious and error-prone, especially when logging entries throughout the day and night. Both parents often end up with out-of-sync data.

Additional issues with current approach:
- Entry ID collisions across devices (numeric IDs generated independently)
- No conflict resolution for concurrent edits
- QR code size limit (~858 bytes) makes full-data sync unreliable
- No awareness of what the other parent has logged

### Proposed Solution

**One-time pairing:**
1. Parent A taps "Create Family" in Settings → app generates a 6-character code (e.g. BLOOM7)
2. Parent A shares the code with Parent B (text, verbally, etc.)
3. Parent B taps "Join Family" → enters the code
4. Both devices are now linked. Sync is automatic from this point forward.

**Automatic real-time sync:**
- All log entries (feeds, diapers, sleep, growth, etc.) sync automatically via Firebase Firestore
- Uses Firestore onSnapshot listeners for real-time push updates
- Works in background — no manual action needed after pairing
- Offline-first: entries queue locally when offline and sync when connectivity returns (Firestore handles this natively)

**End-to-end encryption (E2E):**
- The 6-character pairing code seeds a shared encryption key (derived via PBKDF2 or similar KDF)
- All data is encrypted client-side before writing to Firestore
- Firebase only stores encrypted blobs — cannot read baby names, feed times, growth data, etc.
- Decryption happens on-device only
- This preserves the privacy-first philosophy of the app

**Authentication:**
- Use Firebase Anonymous Auth — each device gets a token silently
- No email, no password, no sign-up friction
- The pairing code links two anonymous accounts to the same family document

### Data Model (Firestore)



- Entry IDs: Firebase auto-generated (globally unique, no collisions)
- Each entry blob decrypts to: { type, date, time, data, deviceId, createdAt }
- The deviceId field identifies which parent logged it

### Migration Path

- Keep IndexedDB as the local cache (offline-first preserved)
- New sync layer sits between React components and the DB
- Writes go to both IndexedDB and Firestore
- Firestore onSnapshot pushes partner changes into IndexedDB
- Existing UI components read from the same local store — minimal UI changes
- The old manual share-code sync can remain as a fallback for users who opt out of cloud sync

### Cost

Firebase Spark (free) tier provides per month:
- 1 GB Firestore storage (BabyBloom would use ~2 MB/year)
- 50,000 reads/day (BabyBloom would use ~200/day)
- 20,000 writes/day (BabyBloom would use ~60/day)
- No credit card required

A single family will never come close to these limits.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Firebase project configured with Firestore + Anonymous Auth
- [ ] "Create Family" flow generates pairing code and encryption key
- [ ] "Join Family" flow accepts code, derives same encryption key
- [ ] All log entries sync automatically between paired devices in <2 seconds on same network
- [ ] Data in Firestore is encrypted and unreadable without the pairing-derived key
- [ ] Offline entries sync when connectivity returns
- [ ] Existing local data migrates into the synced family on first pairing
- [ ] Old manual sync still works as fallback
- [ ] No regression in existing logging functionality

### Testing

- Unit test for encryption/decryption round-trip with pairing-derived key
- Unit test for pairing code generation and family document creation
- Unit test for merge logic when offline entries sync
- Integration test for real-time sync between two simulated devices
- Test that unpaired app continues to work fully offline (no Firebase dependency)
- Test migration of existing IndexedDB data into synced family

---

**Category:** `feature` `sync` `security`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
